About the Studio

Design that starts
with how you live.

Wondurwall Interiors is a Bengaluru-based design studio working across homes, offices, and hospitality spaces. We start with how a space is actually used — light, movement, habit — before a single material is chosen.

Every project moves through the same disciplined process: understand, plan, build, refine. The result is interiors that feel inevitable, never over-designed.

Good interiors aren't decorated. They're resolved. — The Wondurwall Design Philosophy
What We Do

Four ways we shape a space

From a single room to a full commercial fit-out, our services scale to the size of the problem.

Residential Interiors

Complete home design — from layout planning to final styling — for living rooms, bedrooms, kitchens, and every space between.

Commercial Spaces

Offices, retail, and hospitality environments designed around function first — and identity close behind.

Space Planning & Consultation

Spatial audits and layout strategy for spaces that already exist, before a single wall moves.

Custom Furniture & Décor

Bespoke furniture and curated décor pieces, built to finish a room the way it was meant to look.

How We Work

A process built in four moves

The same sequence, every project — because the order is what keeps a design honest.

01

Discover

We visit the space, understand how you live or work, and set a brief.

Site measurement, lifestyle interview, budget and timeline alignment — the foundation every later decision rests on.
02

Design

Concept boards, layouts, and 3D views, refined together with you.

Material palettes, furniture plans, and lighting design are locked before anything is ordered — so surprises happen on paper, not on site.
03

Build

Execution is managed end-to-end, on schedule and on budget.

Vendor coordination, site supervision, and quality checks at every milestone — one point of contact for the entire build.
04

Reveal

Final styling, a walkthrough, and a space that's ready to live in.

Soft furnishings, art, and finishing touches are placed last — then we hand the space back, fully resolved.
